title: Phase 5 — DDGC Town Meta-Loop, Curio Interaction, and Equipment Systems
introduction: Implement the DDGC meta-game loop: town buildings, curio, trap, and obstacle interactions during dungeon runs, and the trinket and equipment systems that drive hero progression so a headless campaign can cycle from town to dungeon and back.
goals: Implement curio, trap, and obstacle interactions during exploration.
Add the town building meta-loop between dungeon runs.
Compute hero power from equipment, trinkets, quirks, diseases, afflictions, and virtues rather than hardcoded stats.

user_stories.title: P5a-0: Define curio, trap, and obstacle data model
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need data structures for curios, traps, and obstacles so the game layer can represent room and corridor interactions beyond combat.
acceptance_criteria: CurioDefinition exists with id, dungeon_scope, results, and item_interactions.
CurioResult captures weight, chance, result_type, and result_id.
ItemInteraction captures item_id and overrides_result_id.
TrapDefinition captures id, success_effects, fail_effects, health_fraction, and difficulty_variations.
ObstacleDefinition captures id, fail_effects, health_fraction, and torchlight_penalty.
All structs derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and Debug.
Focused tests prove struct construction and serialization are deterministic.
notes: Data-model slice only. Match the schemas observed in Curios.csv, Traps.json, and Obstacles.json.

user_stories.title: P5a-1: Parse DDGC curio, trap, and obstacle definitions
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need the DDGC curio, trap, and obstacle data parsed into the data model so actual game content drives interactions.
acceptance_criteria: A CurioRegistry holds all DDGC curio definitions parsed from Curios.csv.
A TrapRegistry holds all DDGC trap definitions parsed from Traps.json.
An ObstacleRegistry holds all DDGC obstacle definitions parsed from Obstacles.json.
At least 3 curios per dungeon region are parsed for QingLong, BaiHu, ZhuQue, and XuanWu.
All 3 trap types and all 5 obstacle types are parsed.
Focused tests prove registry lookup by ID, dungeon-scoped curio filtering, and item interaction overrides are preserved.
notes: Curios.csv uses a multi-row block format per curio and requires custom parsing. Traps.json and Obstacles.json are standard JSON.

user_stories.title: P5a-2: Implement curio interaction resolution
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need a deterministic curio interaction resolver so room exploration produces outcomes matching DDGC's weighted result system.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_curio_interaction(curio_id, has_item, item_id, seed) returns a CurioInteractionOutcome.
Without an item, the resolver selects a result from the weighted table using seeded probability.
With an item, the resolver uses an override result when one exists and otherwise falls back to the weighted table.
CurioInteractionOutcome includes result_type, result_id, and applied_effects.
Focused tests prove weighted bare interaction, item override behavior, and deterministic results for a given seed.
notes: Actual effect application such as applying a quirk is deferred to the relevant subsystem.

user_stories.title: P5a-3: Implement trap and obstacle resolution
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need deterministic trap and obstacle interaction so corridor and room hazards produce correct damage and debuff outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_trap_interaction(trap_id, trap_level, resist_chance, seed) returns TrapOutcome with success or fail effects and HP fraction.
Trap difficulty variations are applied based on dungeon level.
resolve_obstacle_interaction(obstacle_id) returns ObstacleOutcome with fail effects, HP fraction, and torchlight penalty.
Focused tests prove trap success, difficulty-scaled trap failure, and the uniform obstacle penalty pattern.
notes: Traps have a resist or disarm check; obstacles always apply their fail effects.

user_stories.title: P5a-4: Wire curio, trap, and obstacle into room exploration
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need room exploration events to trigger curio, trap, and obstacle interactions so the dungeon run produces the full exploration cycle.
acceptance_criteria: RoomKind::Event rooms can carry a curio_id reference.
RoomKind::Corridor rooms can carry optional trap_id and curio_id.
Entering a room with a curio or obstacle produces an interaction outcome in the run trace.
Traversing a corridor with a trap produces a trap outcome in the run trace.
At least one run slice produces curio interaction and trap interaction entries.
cargo test passes with interaction events in the trace.
notes: Integration slice. Room generation from Phase 4c should assign curios and traps based on DungeonMapConfig densities.

user_stories.title: P5b-0: Define town building data model
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need data structures for town buildings, their upgrade trees, and their services so the game layer can represent the between-run meta state.
acceptance_criteria: TownBuilding tracks id, building_type, unlock_conditions, and upgrade_trees.
UpgradeTree tracks tree_id and levels.
UpgradeLevel tracks code, cost, and building-specific effects.
TownState tracks current upgrade level per building, gold, and heirloom currencies.
All structs derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and Debug.
Focused tests prove TownState construction and upgrade application are deterministic.
notes: Data-model slice. DDGC buildings have variable upgrade structures such as Abbey sub-trees and StageCoach parallel trees.

user_stories.title: P5b-1: Parse DDGC building definitions
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need DDGC building data parsed into the town model so actual building parameters drive the meta loop.
acceptance_criteria: A BuildingRegistry holds all 10 DDGC building definitions parsed from *.building.json files.
Each building has correct unlock conditions, upgrade trees, and service parameters.
At least 3 buildings including StageCoach, Abbey, and Guild are fully parsed with all upgrade levels.
Focused tests prove building lookup, upgrade tree traversal, and loading of all 10 buildings.
notes: Each .building.json has a unique structure, so the parser must handle building-specific fields generically.

user_stories.title: P5b-2: Implement town visit cycle
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need a town visit phase that occurs between dungeon runs so the meta-game loop is complete.
acceptance_criteria: TownVisit represents a single town phase with available services, hero roster state, and gold and heirloom balances.
perform_town_activity(building_id, activity, hero_id, upgrade_level) resolves a building service.
Town visit produces a trace of activities performed.
At least one end-to-end test proves entering town after a dungeon run, performing a stress heal at the Abbey, reducing hero stress, and deducting gold.
Town visit is deterministic for given state and activity choices.
notes: Start with Abbey stress heal and StageCoach recruit as the two most critical buildings.

user_stories.title: P5b-3: Wire town visit into run flow
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need the run flow to include town visits between dungeon runs so the full game loop operates headlessly.
acceptance_criteria: After a dungeon run completes, a town visit phase begins.
Town visit reads hero roster, gold, and heirloom currencies.
Town visit allows at least Abbey stress heal and StageCoach recruit.
Run trace records town activities alongside dungeon run events.
A full-loop run slice from town to dungeon to town completes with heroes progressing between runs.
cargo test passes with the town phase active.
notes: Integration slice. The run loop becomes initialize, town_visit, dungeon_run, town_visit, dungeon_run, and so on until victory or defeat.

user_stories.title: P5b-4: Implement hero XP and leveling
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need hero experience and leveling so heroes can progress through resolve levels between dungeon runs.
acceptance_criteria: HeroProgress tracks xp, resolve_level, and hero_level.
XP thresholds match Campaign.json for level_threshold_table and resolve_thresholds.
add_xp(amount) advances hero level and resolve level when thresholds are crossed.
Resolve level gates dungeon access so heroes above resolve level cannot enter low-level dungeons.
Focused tests prove leveling at each threshold boundary.
notes: DDGC's level system is separate from resolve level. Both use XP from Campaign.json.

user_stories.title: P5c-0: Define trinket and equipment data model
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need data structures for trinkets and equipment upgrades so hero stats can be computed from gear rather than hardcoded values.
acceptance_criteria: TrinketDefinition tracks id, buffs, hero_class_requirements, rarity, price, limit, and origin_dungeon.
EquipmentDefinition tracks id, hero_class_id, slot, upgrade_level, and stat_modifiers.
AttributeModifier tracks attribute_key and value.
All structs derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and Debug.
Focused tests prove construction and lookup are deterministic.
notes: Trinket buffs reference JsonBuffs.json buff IDs that map to attribute modifiers in the game layer.

user_stories.title: P5c-1: Parse DDGC trinket definitions
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need DDGC trinket data parsed into the trinket model so the loot and shop systems can use actual trinket content.
acceptance_criteria: A TrinketRegistry holds all DDGC trinket definitions parsed from JsonTrinkets.json.
Trinkets are indexed by ID, class requirement, rarity, and origin dungeon.
At least 10 trinkets across multiple rarity tiers are parsed and verified.
Class-requirement filtering works so trinkets_for_class returns only eligible trinkets.
Focused tests prove lookup, filtering, and rarity categorization.
notes: JsonTrinkets.json references buff IDs from JsonBuffs.json; the buff-resolution layer is a separate concern.

user_stories.title: P5c-2: Implement buff resolution from trinket references
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need trinket buff references resolved to concrete attribute modifiers so equipped trinkets modify hero stats correctly.
acceptance_criteria: A BuffRegistry maps DDGC buff IDs to attribute modifiers.
Buff IDs follow DDGC naming conventions with stat abbreviations and sign or suffix conventions.
resolve_buffs(trinket) returns all AttributeModifier entries for that trinket's buff list.
At least 5 buff IDs are resolved to correct attribute modifications.
Focused tests prove positive and negative modifiers, percentage-based versus flat modifiers, and multi-buff aggregation.
notes: Need a buff ID to modifier parser for mixed naming such as MAXHP-15, GIVE_STRESSDMG20, DMGL-25, and DMGH-25.

user_stories.title: P5c-3: Compute hero stats from equipment
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need hero combat stats computed from base, equipment, and trinkets instead of hardcoded values so the equipment progression system changes hero power.
acceptance_criteria: compute_hero_stats(hero_class, weapon_level, armor_level, trinkets) returns a full attribute map.
Level-0 equipment with no trinkets produces the same stats as current hardcoded values.
Higher weapon levels increase ATK and higher armor levels increase DEF or HP.
Equipping a trinket applies its resolved modifiers to the attribute map.
Focused tests prove level 0 equals current stats, level 1 weapon exceeds level 0 weapon ATK, and a trinket modifies the correct attribute.
notes: Parse upgrade data from Upgrades/Heroes/ JSON files.

user_stories.title: P5c-4: Wire equipment into hero archetype creation
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need hero archetype creation to accept equipment parameters so the same hero class can have different power levels based on gear.
acceptance_criteria: Archetype creation for heroes accepts optional weapon_level, armor_level, and trinkets parameters.
The default path without parameters produces current level-0 stats.
All existing tests continue passing without modification.
At least one test proves a level-1 equipped hero has different stats than level-0.
cargo test passes with equipment-aware archetype creation.
notes: This is the point where hero stats stop being hardcoded and start being computed.

user_stories.title: P5d-0: Define quirk data model and parse DDGC quirks
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need quirk definitions parsed from JsonQuirks.json so heroes can acquire and be affected by quirks and diseases.
acceptance_criteria: QuirkDefinition tracks id, is_positive, is_disease, classification, buffs, incompatible_quirks, and curio_tag.
A QuirkRegistry holds all DDGC quirk definitions.
At least 10 quirks including positive, negative, and disease entries are parsed.
Buffs are resolved through the BuffRegistry from US-913.
Focused tests prove lookup, positive or negative or disease classification, and buff resolution.
notes: Quirks use the same buff ID system as trinkets and depend on the BuffRegistry.

user_stories.title: P5d-1: Implement quirk application to heroes
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need heroes to carry active quirks that modify their attributes so the quirk system actually affects gameplay.
acceptance_criteria: HeroQuirkState tracks active positive quirks, negative quirks, and diseases.
apply_quirk(hero, quirk_id) adds a quirk while enforcing incompatible quirk replacement and category slot limits.
Quirk modifiers are applied alongside equipment and trinket modifiers in compute_hero_stats.
Focused tests prove quirk modifiers change hero attributes, incompatible quirks replace existing ones, and diseases are tracked separately.
notes: DDGC typically limits heroes to five positive and five negative quirks.

user_stories.title: P5d-2: Wire quirk acquisition into curio and combat events
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need quirks to be acquired from curio interactions and combat events so the hero's quirk profile evolves during runs.
acceptance_criteria: Curio result type Quirk triggers apply_quirk with the result quirk ID.
Curio result type Disease triggers apply_quirk with the disease quirk ID.
Combat events that cause disease trigger apply_quirk.
At least one end-to-end test proves a curio adds a quirk, disease from combat is applied, and quirk modifiers affect subsequent combat stats.
cargo test passes with quirk acquisition active.
notes: Integration slice connecting the curio system, combat system, and quirk system.

user_stories.title: P5e-0: Define trait and affliction data model and parse DDGC traits
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need trait definitions for afflictions and virtues parsed from JsonTraits.json so the overstress system can resolve correctly.
acceptance_criteria: TraitDefinition tracks id, overstress_type, buff_ids, combat_start_turn_act_outs, and reaction_act_outs.
ActOutEntry tracks action and weight.
ReactionEntry tracks trigger, probability, and effect.
A TraitRegistry holds all DDGC trait definitions.
At least 2 afflictions and 1 virtue are parsed.
Focused tests prove trait lookup, act-out weight preservation, and reaction probability preservation.
notes: JsonTraits.json uses nested arrays for act-outs and reactions.

user_stories.title: P5e-1: Implement overstress resolution
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need overstress resolution implemented so heroes who exceed max stress get an affliction or virtue instead of being stuck at max stress.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_overstress(hero_id, seed) rolls between affliction and virtue outcomes.
Affliction selection uses DDGC's weighted trait table.
The selected trait is applied to the hero as an active affliction or virtue.
Trait buffs modify hero attributes through the same modifier pipeline as quirks and trinkets.
Focused tests prove affliction is common, virtue is possible but rare, and trait buffs modify hero stats.
notes: Use seeded RNG for deterministic resolution.

user_stories.title: P5e-2: Implement affliction combat behavior
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need affliction combat behavior implemented so afflicted heroes occasionally disobey orders during combat.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_act_out(affliction_id, seed) rolls against the weighted act-out table at the start of an afflicted hero's turn.
Possible outcomes include Nothing, IgnoreCommand, ChangePosition, BarkStress, AttackFriendly, and MarkSelf.
Act-out results are recorded in the battle trace.
Focused tests prove Nothing is the most common outcome, other outcomes are possible, and act-out resolution is deterministic for a given seed.
notes: DDGC's act-out weights make nothing dominant with smaller chances for disruptive actions.

user_stories.title: P5e-3: Wire overstress into battle loop
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need the battle loop to check for overstress and resolve it so the stress mechanic produces consequences during combat.
acceptance_criteria: When a hero's stress exceeds ATTR_MAX_STRESS, resolve_overstress is called.
The resulting affliction or virtue takes effect immediately with buffs applied and act-outs enabled.
Battle trace records overstress events with the resulting trait.
At least one end-to-end test proves stress damage pushes a hero over threshold, affliction is applied, and act-out occurs on a subsequent turn.
cargo test passes with overstress resolution active.
notes: Integration slice completing the stress to affliction to combat behavior chain.
